{"society_power": 4.5, "state_power": 2.5, "society_change": 1.0, "state_change": -0.5, "key_event": "Boxer defeat, exam abolition, Tongmenghui, provincial assemblies", "reasoning": "The Boxer catastrophe, crushing indemnity, examination abolition, and 1908 leadership vacuum eroded Qing legitimacy and finances despite Xinzheng reforms, nudging state power down; meanwhile nationalist networks, a modern press, revolutionary organizations, and new provincial assemblies substantially strengthened an assertive society and provincial elites."}
